seo_h1: Artemon of Cassandreia on the Collection and Use of Books

seo.browser_title: The Artemon of Cassandreia on Eulogikon Greek Archive
seo.meta_title: The Classical Artemon of Cassandreia Ancient Greek Works
seo.meta_description: Artemon of Cassandreia, Hellenistic grammarian of the 4th century BCE. Surviving grammatical treatise. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Artemon of Cassandreia was a grammarian and literary critic active in the late 4th and early 3rd centuries BCE. A native of the Macedonian city of Cassandreia, he was a contemporary and associate of Neleus of Scepsis, a Peripatetic scholar who inherited Aristotle's library. While sometimes confused with a later Artemon of Pergamon, the earlier grammarian is consistently linked to Cassandreia.

His known work is the compilation and publication of an edition of Aristotle’s letters, an editorial project now lost. Artemon’s significance rests on his role as an early editor of Aristotelian texts. His collection of Aristotle's correspondence represents an early effort to systematize the philosopher's works, linking him to the crucial early transmission history of Aristotle's library through his association with Neleus.

title.english: On the Collection and Use of Books
